title: Refresh Context Status Color
prompt:
# TASK 1: Refresh Context Status Color

## CONTEXT
The Refresh Context button works but shows yellow status text. It should show white.

## CURRENT BEHAVIOR
In renderer.js line ~960, refreshContext() shows:
- "Context refreshed" in yellow (#dcdcaa)

## REQUIRED CHANGE
Change status color to white (#ffffff) for "Context refreshed" message.

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/renderer/renderer.js

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. npm start
2. Create a model via chat
3. Click Refresh Context button (or Edit menu)
4. Status bar shows "Context refreshed" in WHITE text
5. After 2 seconds, returns to "Ready" in grey

This is a one-line fix.

title: Persistent Context Usage Display
prompt:
# TASK 2: Persistent Context Usage Display

## CONTEXT
Users need to know when Claude's context is filling up so they can proactively refresh.

IMPORTANT: Calling /context costs ~1k tokens per call. Do NOT poll after every exchange.

## APPROACH: Local Estimation + Smart Verification

### Step 1: Estimate Locally (Free)
After each Claude exchange, estimate tokens client-side:
```javascript
function estimateTokens() {
  // ~4 characters per token (rough estimate)
  const codeTokens = currentCode.length / 4;
  const historyTokens = messageHistory
    .reduce((sum, msg) => sum + msg.content.length, 0) / 4;
  const systemPromptTokens = 3000; // Our system prompt ~3k
  
  return Math.round(codeTokens + historyTokens + systemPromptTokens);
}
```

### Step 2: Auto-Verify at 70%
When local estimate reaches 70% of 200k (140k tokens):
- Automatically call /context to get real usage
- Store the actual value and calibration offset

### Step 3: Calibrate
```javascript
let calibrationOffset = 0;
function calibrate(estimated, actual) {
  calibrationOffset = actual - estimated;
}
function getCalibratedEstimate() {
  return estimateTokens() + calibrationOffset;
}
```

### Step 4: Re-verify Every 5k
After initial verification, re-verify every ~5k tokens (locally calculated) to stay accurate.

## DISPLAY
- Show in status bar: "Context: ~45%" (tilde indicates estimate)
- After verification: "Context: 72%" (no tilde, verified)
- Color coding:
  - Below 70%: Grey (#888888) or hidden
  - 70-79%: Yellow (#dcdcaa)
  - 80-89%: Orange (#ff9900)
  - 90%+: Bright red (#ff4444)
- Persists (doesn't auto-hide)

## STATE TO TRACK
```javascript
let lastVerifiedTokens = 0;      // Last /context result
let lastVerifiedEstimate = 0;    // Our estimate at verification time
let calibrationOffset = 0;       // Difference (actual - estimated)
let tokensAtLastVerify = 0;      // For 5k re-verify trigger
let hasVerified = false;         // Whether we've verified at least once
```

## IMPORTANT: Silent Operation
The /context verification calls are INTERNAL ONLY:
- Do NOT add to chat history (messageHistory array)
- Do NOT show in chat UI
- User only sees the status bar percentage update
- This is background housekeeping, invisible to user

## TOKEN MATH: Include Hidden Exchanges
- Token estimate MUST include hidden exchange tokens (like /context calls ~1k each)
- Track separately: `hiddenExchangeTokens` (session-only variable)
- Add to estimate: `estimateTokens() + hiddenExchangeTokens + calibrationOffset`
- Do NOT save hiddenExchangeTokens to project file
- On project load: reset hiddenExchangeTokens to 0 (fresh context)

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/main/claude-manager.js (add context query method - separate from chat)
- src/main/main.js (add IPC handler)
- src/renderer/index.html (add display element)
- src/renderer/renderer.js (estimation + verification logic)
- src/renderer/styles.css (styling)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. npm start
2. Send messages - see "Context: ~5%" (grey, estimated)
3. Keep chatting until ~70%
4. Auto-verifies, display updates to "Context: 72%" (yellow, verified)
5. Every ~5k more tokens, re-verifies automatically
6. Colors escalate: yellow -> orange -> red

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Pulsing Red Model During Processing
prompt:
# TASK 3: Pulsing Red Model During Processing

## CONTEXT
Replace the spinner overlay with a more elegant visual: the model pulses red while Claude is thinking.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Remove/hide the spinner overlay during Claude exchanges
2. When processing starts ('claude' phase):
   - Dim the model to a dark red color
   - Animate pulsing: smoothly oscillate between dim red and lighter red
   - Use requestAnimationFrame for smooth animation
3. When processing ends:
   - Stop animation
   - Restore original model color(s)
4. If no model exists yet, fall back to status bar indicator only

## ANIMATION DETAILS
- Base color: dim red (~#661111 or similar)
- Pulse to: lighter red (~#aa3333)
- Pulse speed: ~1-2 second cycle
- Smooth sine wave interpolation

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Store original material colors before starting
- Create animation loop that modifies material.color
- Clean up animation on stop
- Handle multi-mesh models (traverse and animate all)

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/renderer/renderer.js (animation logic, modify setProcessing)
- src/renderer/styles.css (hide spinner if needed)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. npm start, create a model
2. Send a new message to Claude
3. Model turns red and pulses smoothly
4. When response arrives, model returns to normal color
5. No spinner visible during this process

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Disable Actions During Processing
prompt:
# TASK 4: Disable Actions During Processing

## CONTEXT
While Claude is processing, destructive actions should be disabled but view controls should remain active.

## ACTIONS TO DISABLE DURING PROCESSING
- Save (Cmd+S)
- Save As
- Load/Open (Cmd+O)
- Export STL (Cmd+E)
- Undo (Cmd+Z)
- Redo (Cmd+Shift+Z)
- Clear Project (Cmd+Shift+K)
- Refresh Context
- New Project

## ACTIONS TO KEEP ENABLED
- Camera orbit/pan/zoom (TrackballControls)
- View presets dropdown (Front, Top, Isometric, etc.)
- Measure tool (M key and button)
- Grid toggle (when implemented)
- Axes toggle (when implemented)
- Escape key (clear selection/measurement)

## IMPLEMENTATION
1. Add `isProcessing` check to each disabled action
2. Show brief status message if user tries disabled action: "Please wait..."
3. Disable toolbar buttons visually (greyed out)
4. Menu items should also be disabled (via IPC to main process)

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/renderer/renderer.js (add isProcessing checks)
- src/main/main.js (menu enable/disable via IPC)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Send a message to Claude
2. While pulsing red, try Cmd+S -> "Please wait..." in status
3. Camera controls still work (can orbit the pulsing model)
4. Measure tool still works
5. When processing ends, all actions re-enabled

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Preserve Material Colors in Renderer
Export Colors from Build123d
prompt:
# TASK 6: Export Colors from Build123d

## CONTEXT
Build123d needs to export per-feature colors to glTF so renderer can display them.

## RESEARCH NEEDED
1. Check if Build123d's export_gltf preserves colors
2. Check how to assign colors to shapes in Build123d
3. Test with a simple multi-body model

## BUILD123D COLOR ASSIGNMENT
Build123d shapes can have colors assigned. Research the API:
- Part.color property?
- Compound with colored children?
- export_gltf options for materials?

## TEST CODE
```python
from build123d import *

# Create two spheres with different colors
with BuildPart() as part:
    with Locations((0, 0, 0)):
        Sphere(10)
    with Locations((25, 0, 0)):
        Sphere(8)

# How to assign colors?
# Export and check if colors appear in glTF
```

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/python/cad_engine.py (if export changes needed)
- System prompt (if Claude needs to know color syntax)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Create multi-body model with colors in Build123d
2. Export to glTF
3. Load in Three.js
4. Each body has its assigned color

## NOTE
This may require research. If Build123d doesn't support per-body colors in export, document the limitation and we'll handle colors client-side only.

Provide findings and plan before implementing.

title: Per-Feature Click Detection
Per-Feature Hover Highlight
Color Palette UI
prompt:
# TASK 9: Color Palette UI

## CONTEXT
When a feature is selected (clicked), show a color palette so user can change that feature's color.

## COLOR PALETTE
9 colors:
- Black (#000000)
- White (#ffffff)
- Blue (#4a9eff)
- Red (#ff4444)
- Yellow (#ffff00)
- Green (#44ff44)
- Cyan (#00ffff)
- Magenta (#ff00ff)
- Orange (#ff9900)

## UI DESIGN
1. Small popup/toolbar appears near the clicked point or in a fixed location
2. Shows 9 color swatches as clickable squares
3. Current color has a border/highlight
4. Clicking a color applies it immediately
5. Clicking outside or pressing Escape closes palette
6. Palette only visible when a feature is selected

## IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS
A) Floating popup near click point
B) Toolbar section that appears when feature selected
C) Right-click context menu

Recommend option B (toolbar) for simplicity.

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/renderer/index.html (palette HTML)
- src/renderer/styles.css (palette styling)
- src/renderer/renderer.js (show/hide logic, click handlers)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Click on a feature
2. Color palette appears
3. Click a color -> feature changes to that color
4. Click elsewhere -> palette hides
5. Palette shows which color is currently active

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Apply Color and Log to Chat
Save/Load Feature Colors
Grid Toggle
prompt:
# TASK 12: Grid Toggle

## CONTEXT
Users should be able to show/hide the XY grid.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add Grid toggle button to toolbar (icon: grid pattern)
2. Default: grid visible
3. Click toggles visibility
4. Button shows active state when grid is visible
5. Optional: keyboard shortcut (G key)

## IMPLEMENTATION
```javascript
// Grid is already created as gridHelper
let gridVisible = true;

function toggleGrid() {
  gridVisible = !gridVisible;
  gridHelper.visible = gridVisible;
  // Update button state
  document.getElementById('grid-button').classList.toggle('active', gridVisible);
}
```

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/renderer/index.html (add button)
- src/renderer/renderer.js (toggle logic)
- src/renderer/styles.css (button styling if needed)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. npm start
2. Grid visible by default, button shows active
3. Click Grid button -> grid disappears, button inactive
4. Click again -> grid reappears

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Axes Toggle
prompt:
# TASK 13: Axes Toggle

## CONTEXT
Users should be able to show/hide the XYZ axes and labels.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add Axes toggle button to toolbar (icon: XYZ arrows)
2. Default: axes visible
3. Click toggles visibility of:
   - axesHelper (the RGB lines)
   - labelX, labelY, labelZ (the letter sprites)
4. Button shows active state when axes visible
5. Optional: keyboard shortcut (A key)

## IMPLEMENTATION
```javascript
let axesVisible = true;

function toggleAxes() {
  axesVisible = !axesVisible;
  axesHelper.visible = axesVisible;
  labelX.visible = axesVisible;
  labelY.visible = axesVisible;
  labelZ.visible = axesVisible;
  document.getElementById('axes-button').classList.toggle('active', axesVisible);
}
```

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/renderer/index.html (add button)
- src/renderer/renderer.js (toggle logic)
- src/renderer/styles.css (button styling if needed)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. npm start
2. Axes and labels visible by default
3. Click Axes button -> axes and labels disappear
4. Click again -> they reappear

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Status Bar Model Stats
prompt:
# TASK 17: Status Bar Model Stats

## CONTEXT
Show model statistics in the status bar: face count and volume.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Display in right side of status bar: "Faces: 1,234 | Volume: 5.67 cm3"
2. Update after each model load
3. Clear when no model loaded
4. Face count from Three.js geometry
5. Volume from Python/Build123d (more accurate)

## FACE COUNT (Three.js)
```javascript
let faceCount = 0;
currentMesh.traverse((child) => {
  if (child.isMesh && child.geometry) {
    const positions = child.geometry.attributes.position;
    faceCount += positions.count / 3; // 3 vertices per triangle
  }
});
```

## VOLUME (Python)
Add to cad_engine.py response:
```python
volume = part.part.volume  # Build123d provides this
# Return in response JSON
```

## FILES TO MODIFY
- src/renderer/index.html (add stats display element)
- src/renderer/renderer.js (calculate and display)
- src/python/cad_engine.py (return volume)
- src/main/main.js (pass volume through IPC)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Create a box
2. Status bar shows: "Faces: 12 | Volume: 8.00 cm3" (for 20x20x20 box)
3. Create complex model -> numbers update
4. Clear project -> stats disappear

Provide a plan before implementing code.
